zotero.abstractNote: Mixed-integer programming (MIP) is a powerful tool for solving hybrid model predictive control (HMPC) problems. However, the computational complexity of MIP is NP-hard, which often prevents its application in real-time control. In this paper, we propose a fast non-parametric learning approach to accelerate the solution of HMPC problems. We learn a mapping from the state to the optimal integer sequence of the MIP problem. By predicting the optimal integer sequence, the MIP problem reduces to a convex quadratic program (QP), which can be solved very efficiently. We use a k-nearest neighbor (k-NN) classifier for the learning task, which is non-parametric and allows for fast training and inference. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on a hybrid system benchmark problem, showing significant speedups compared to standard MIP solvers while maintaining high control performance.
